title: SCBS 0.5 service target
rationale: SCBS is already operational at the engine level. The next highest-value work is not more planner depth but service ownership: operator/admin HTTP APIs for diagnostics and queue control, persistent background worker execution with retry semantics, and a service-grade standalone operations baseline. Those three pieces are the shortest path from usable engine to dependable standalone service for swarm use.

name: scbs-0-6-admin-worker-surface
description: SCBS 0.6 service ownership should share one durable job model across CLI, HTTP admin APIs, SDK, and PostgreSQL state: expose doctor/job inspection over /api/v1/admin, make repo scan and receipt validation queueable, add retry-aware job fields (attempts, maxAttempts, availableAt, lastError, started/completed timestamps), and run persistent worker loops over the same drain primitive rather than inventing a separate execution path.

title: SCBS 0.7 target
rationale: After SCBS 0.6 established a real standalone service boundary with admin APIs and persistent worker execution, the next highest-value move toward the SCBS.md 1.0 product is to make the system reviewable, auditable, and integratable. The 0.7 slice therefore prioritizes bundle/receipt review surfaces, durable outbox or webhook contracts, and scoped audit logging rather than deeper core-engine work.

name: scbs-0-7-review-outbox-audit-surface
description: SCBS 0.7 should reuse one durable service state across CLI, HTTP, SDK, and PostgreSQL for bundle review visibility, receipt review history, lifecycle outbox events, webhook subscriptions, and audit records instead of splitting those concerns across separate stores.

title: SCBS 0.7 scoped access at the HTTP boundary
rationale: Persist scoped service tokens in SCBS state and enforce admin/repo scopes in the HTTP handler, while recording durable audit entries for successful and denied admin/repository requests so both local JSON and PostgreSQL modes behave the same.

content: README.md for SCBS service milestones should be operator-first and adoption-first: document the actual deployment bootstrap, HTTP integration path, auth bootstrap behavior, worker requirements, and concrete curl/CLI examples for agent swarm use instead of generic workspace scaffolding.

title: Mission Control should operate SCBS as a managed sibling service to SISU
rationale: For the integrated 24/7 platform, SCBS should start automatically with Mission Control/SISU and remain separated by API/SDK instead of being installed ad hoc into each project. SCBS needs durable shared state, a worker process, and stable service ownership, so npm packaging should remain secondary and focus on SDK/bootstrap tooling rather than being the primary runtime model.

name: scbs-platform-up-local-launcher
description: For local integrated development, SCBS should provide a single parent launcher that ensures PostgreSQL, runs migrations, starts the SCBS API, waits for health, then starts the SCBS worker and optional SISU or Mission Control commands from env-provided shell commands. The launcher is a development orchestrator, not a substitute for production service supervision.
